mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations. A month-over-month drop in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days as market reprices equity risk premium downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% as of 2023-03-15 suggests easing cycle underway
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive FEDFUNDS changes fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, equity valuations compress due to earnings recession expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer demand strength and future earnings visibility. Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales drives positive equity returns as investors increase growth expectations. This is an always-on trend signal with 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 483895 million dollars as of 2023-03-15
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals real economic capacity utilization and corporate profitability. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it indicates supply-side weakness and margin compression, producing negative equity returns within 5 trading days as market reprices earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative INDPRO momentum, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal demand-side momentum and labor market health. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, construction and employment multipliers amplify economic activity, producing positive 20-day equity returns. The mechanism requires both signals to be active.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1180.0 thousand units and PAYEMS at 146659.0 thousand persons as of 2023-03-15
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive signals fail to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS level, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation. This regime produces positive equity returns as investors seek real asset hedges. The mechanism is disabled when real rates turn positive.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 246.373 and FEDFUNDS at 1.15% as of 2023-03-15
regime_hint: negative real rates or monetary accommodation regime
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative real rate regime fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth rate decelerates when labor demand weakens ahead of recession. A 3-month moving average of PAYEMS growth falling below 100k monthly additions signals earnings recession risk, producing negative 20-day equity returns as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS growth < 100k additions per month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146659.0 thousand persons as of 2023-03-15
regime_hint: labor market deceleration or pre-recession period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances despite weak payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure demand momentum. When both series show positive quarter-over-quarter growth with RSAFS accelerating, consumer-led expansion drives equity valuations higher. This interaction-driven mechanism requires synchronized demand signals to activate.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 positive QoQ growth and RSAFS 3-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17031.1 billion chained 2017 dollars and RSAFS at 483895 million dollars as of 2023-03-15
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion or sustained growth regime
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or synchronized demand signals fail to predict positive 60-day returns in > 56% of instances.
